strengths: 20 years of continuous software engineering experience across diverse technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, AWS, Kubernetes) with demonstrated Staff/Senior leadership roles
Proven ability to build and maintain platforms (GISCollective open-source project, contributor to multiple infrastructure initiatives)
Strong knowledge-sharing and leadership track record through workshops, talks (FOSS4G, TDT), and community mentoring (Refactoring Dojos)
concerns: AI expertise is recent and limited (~1.5 years at re:cinq in MCP/agentic infrastructure) with no demonstrated AI products or platforms built—critical gap for an AI Strategy role
No Master's or PhD in AI/ML, which is a preferred qualification; both degrees are in general CS and design, not AI-specialized

Background is generalist across many domains (GIS, direct mail, robotics, media, fintech) rather than specialized expertise in cloud and AI platforms, and lacks evidence of strategic customer advisory or platform vision work

key_factors.factor: Technical depth and breadth across 20 years
Strategic and advisory positioning for principal role

response.justification: This candidate possesses strong technical foundations and 20 years of experience across diverse engineering domains, but falls short of the core requirements for a Principal Engineer in AI Strategy. The role explicitly requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI,' yet this candidate has only ~1.5 years in AI infrastructure tooling with no evidence of AI products or strategic customer advisory work. The lack of an advanced AI/ML degree compounds this gap. While the candidate's generalist background and recent entry into AI infrastructure shows potential for growth, they currently lack the domain specialization and strategic positioning expected at a director-level AI strategy role.
